title: How AI is diagnosing diseases faster than radiologists now
post:
A radiologist reviews about 100 cases a day. AI systems are reviewing thousands simultaneously, flagging anomalies with accuracy rates that are starting to exceed human specialists in specific domains.

This isn't about replacing doctors. It's about giving them superpowers. AI handles the screening — identifying which cases need immediate attention, catching things that might be missed in a long shift, processing images faster. Then the radiologist applies clinical judgment that AI simply cannot replicate.

The practices that are adopting these tools now are going to provide better care at lower cost. The ones that don't are going to fall behind.

What do you think about AI in medical diagnosis? Thoughts?
